page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 640 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at 2.2 GHz. It features 1360.0 GB of dedicated system memory, translating to 14.17 GB per core, and includes 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance dichotomy between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core CPU performance is weak, as evidenced by low stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, alongside bottom-tier single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core and parallelized execution is highly robust. The server achieves strong ratings in multi-core stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and clang software compilation benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, scoring in the top tier for both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached read/write operations.

The primary strength of this server lies in its GPU-accelerated capabilities, particularly for large language model (LLM) workloads. It achieves top-tier performance in prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, as well as top-tier text generation speeds for medium and large models. Ray tracing benchmarks also register in the top 25%.

Given the high density of specialized hardware, including eight enterprise GPUs and substantial system memory, this server represents a high-capital resource class. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for massive parallel processing tasks where GPU utilization remains high, but inefficient for general-purpose or single-threaded workloads. Best-fit workloads include large-scale LLM training and inference, deep learning, HPC, and complex data science pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-density parallel computing. It features eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB of total VRAM, paired with 96 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture and 1360.0 GB of system memory. Local storage is provided by 3216 GB of NVMe SSDs. While single-core CPU benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the bottom tier, the server delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth. Its primary strength is GPU-accelerated workloads, achieving top-tier results in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes. This hardware profile is optimized for demanding workloads such as large language model training, deep learning, and HPC, offering high resource density for parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-8g features 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 vCPUs, and 1360GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference and multi-core parallel computing workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server with 8 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 vCPUs, 1360GB RAM, and 3216GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring eight enterprise GPUs and massive memory bandwidth for parallel computing and large language model inference.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB total VRAM for parallel acceleration.
96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1360 GB of system memory.
3216 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier benchmark performance in LLM prompt processing and text generation.
Strong multi-core execution and high-bandwidth memory read/write capabilities.
Optimized for deep learning, LLM training, and HPC.
